summary: Linked `itkirbycutterbeam.c` by adding its item state table and switching the object from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`. The PR also filled in previously missed item work for Ness yoyo and Ice Climbers string articles, replacing several `/// #...` stubs and `UNK` prototypes with typed implementations. decomp.dev reported +1908 matched code bytes, +2588 linked code bytes, +56 matched data bytes, 8 new matches, and 10 near-match improvements.

key_files.role: Added `ItemStateTable it_803F6798[]` for Kirby cutter beam and removed now-unneeded broad placeholder/platform/type includes.
Implemented multiple Ness yoyo motion, chain, collision, cleanup, and matrix helper functions.
Replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` or vague prototypes with concrete `ItemLink*`, `Vec3*`, `itYoyoAttributes*`, `Item*`, and `Item_GObj*` signatures.
Implemented Ice Climbers string chain update, cleanup, and motion callbacks.
Moved/added declarations for Climbers string helpers and exposed typed `ItemLink`/attribute helper prototypes.
Split `itClimbersStringAttributes` padding to expose `f32 x8` and `f32 x14` while preserving later offsets.

what_changed: `configure.py` now treats `melee/it/items/itkirbycutterbeam.c` as Matching instead of NonMatching.

`itkirbycutterbeam.c` gained `ItemStateTable it_803F6798[] = { NULL, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Anim, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Coll }`, which decomp.dev reported as a new `.data` match worth +56 bytes.
`itCharItems.h` refined `itClimbersStringAttributes`: the old `pad_00[0x18]` became `pad_00[0x8]`, `f32 x8`, `pad_0C[0x8]`, and `f32 x14`, leaving `x18`, `x1C`, and `x20` at their documented offsets.
`itclimbersstring.c` added an inline cleanup helper that notifies `ftPp_SpecialS_8012114C`, clears owner/item-var links, and destroys each linked segment gobj with `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228`.
`itclimbersstring.h` gained typed prototypes for the chain helpers and motion callback, including `ItemLink*`, `Vec3*`, `itClimbersStringAttributes*`, and `Item*` parameters.
`itnessyoyo.c` added includes for `ft/ftlib.h`, `it/items/itlinkhookshot.h`, `mp/mpcoll.h`, and `MSL/math_ppc.h` to support typed fighter scaling, link-chain, collision, and math logic.
`itnessyoyo.h` replaced ambiguous signatures for `it_802BF030`, `it_802BF180`, `it_802BF28C`, `it_802BFEC4`, and `it_802C0010` with concrete typed declarations.
The PR body says this also matched item files missed in the previous PR; no further human rationale was present in the slice.
smart_moves: Linked Kirby cutter beam by fixing both the build configuration and the missing `.data` state table, rather than only flipping the configure flag.
Reused the existing `ItemLink` abstraction via `itlinkhookshot.h` for yoyo/string segment chains, making prev/next traversal and segment cleanup explicit.
Unlocked Climbers string physics by identifying real floats inside `itClimbersStringAttributes` at offsets `+08` and `+14` instead of leaving the whole leading region as padding.
Converted unknown header prototypes to typed `ItemLink`/`Vec3`/attribute signatures, which documents ABI expectations and helps nearby files call helpers without `UNK_PARAMS`.

Matched repeated article-endpoint code with the same matrix pattern: identity matrix, translation setup, `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix`, `PSMTXConcat`, then extracting `m[0][3]`, `m[1][3]`, and `m[2][3]` into a `Vec3`.
Kept owner-action lifetime checks local to animation callbacks: Ness yoyo uses `ftLib_80086C0C` range `0x156..0x15B`, while Climbers string uses `0x15B..0x164`.
Used inline cleanup helpers for the article-gobj destruction loops, preserving item owner cleanup behavior while keeping the public motion callbacks focused on whether cleanup should happen.
decomp_lessons: For item articles, object linking may depend on small data declarations such as `ItemStateTable` arrays; check `.data` before marking a file Matching in `configure.py`.
Do not leave attribute structs as large padding when functions reveal accessed offsets; exposing `f32` fields by offset can unblock physics/chain code and improve matching.

Rope-like item articles share reusable patterns: `ItemLink` prev/next traversal, `x2C_b0` segment-active flags, per-segment velocity/collision updates, and gobj destruction through `HSD_GObjPLink_80390228`.
Typed headers are part of decomp progress: replacing `UNK_RET` and `UNK_PARAMS` with concrete signatures can stabilize call sites and make later matching easier.

Animation callbacks for spawned fighter articles often need to verify the owning fighter is still in the expected action-state range, then clear ownership and notify the fighter-specific special-move code if not.
Near matches are still valuable: the bot reported several 91-99% improvements in yoyo and Climbers string functions even though only a subset became exact matches in this PR.
naming_conventions: Preserved item state naming pattern: `itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll` in `it_803F6798`.
Used address-based names for unnamed item helpers, e.g. `it_802BF030`, `it_802BF180`, `it_802C2CA8`, and `fn_802C28DC`.
Kept project motion callback names such as `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion1_Phys`, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion2_Phys`, and `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim`.
Used the existing exact casing `itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim` and cleanup helper `itClimbersstring_Cleanup` rather than normalizing to a different capitalization.
Unknown struct fields remained offset-style names (`x8`, `x14`, `x18`, `x1C`, `x20`) with explicit padding names like `pad_00` and `pad_0C`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted local padding arrays such as `f32 pad[1]`, `pad_top`, `pad_mid`, and `pad_bottom` in several functions to shape stack layout.
Left `PAD_STACK(8)` in `itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim`, indicating stack-size/codegen sensitivity even after the visible return path.

Repeated matrix extraction order was important: initialize `Mtx`, write translation constants, call `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix`, concatenate with the jobj matrix, then copy translation components into `Vec3`.
Used `it_804DD150` as the zero-like float constant for Ness yoyo matrix translations, while Climbers string used literal `0.0f`.
For yoyo collision, `it_802BF030` updates `CollData` positions, calls `mpColl_800471F8`, checks `env_flags` masks `0x18000`, `0xFFF`, and returns `env_flags & 0x18FFF`.

For chain shortening, both yoyo and Climbers string walk backward through `prev`, skip inactive links via `x2C_b0`, compute distance with `it_802A3C98`, clamp the step, then call the chain-relaxation helper.

`it_802BF900` applies owner scale from `ftLib_80086A0C`, composes translation/scale matrices, copies to a target JObj, ORs `JOBJ_USER_DEF_MTX | JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_PARENT | JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT`, and marks the matrix dirty.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev reported for GALE01: matched code 63.33% (+0.05%, +1908 bytes), linked code 34.42% (+0.07%, +2588 bytes), matched data 38.14% (+56 bytes), linked data 32.45% (+136 bytes).

decomp.dev listed 10 improvements in unmatched items, including `itnessyoyo` `it_802BF900` at 99.81%, `itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Anim` at 98.04%, `it_802BFEC4` at 99.98%, `it_802BF030` at 97.50%, and Climbers string helpers in the 91-99% range.
follow_up_queries: Find the previous PR referenced by the body text to see what item files were missed and whether patterns carry over.
Investigate why `it_802C2CA8`, `it_802C2DB0`, `it_802C32D4`, and `itClimbersstring_UnkMotion3_Anim` were only near-matches in the bot report.
Audit `ItemLink` fields used here (`prev`, `next`, `pos`, `vel`, `x2C_b0`, `x2C_b1`, `x2C_b2`) for better names across hookshot, yoyo, and Climbers string articles.
Search for remaining fighter-related item files still marked NonMatching near `itkirbycutterbeam.c`, especially `itlinkbomb.c`.
